@graph.name: Mercedes CLA35 AMG Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes CLA35 AMG engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M260 engine typically costs £4,500–£7,000 supply only, with rebuilt units reaching £8,000. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800. A new engine from a Mercedes main dealer can exceed £10,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your CLA35 AMG.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes CLA35 AMG (M260) engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M260 is considered reliable in its early years of production, benefiting from modern materials, cooling and precise ECU management. However, it has known weaknesses: timing chain tensioner failure (rattling on cold start), cylinder head/valve guide wear (misfires, loss of compression), and Camtronic system failures. Regular maintenance with Mercedes‑approved oil is essential.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes CLA35 AMG engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner failure (rattling on cold start), cylinder head/valve guide wear (misfires, loss of compression), Camtronic system failure (loss of low‑end power), oil leaks, and carbon buildup on intake valves.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a CLA35 AMG engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. A CLA35 AMG is a £15,000–£35,000 vehicle — a £5,000–£6,000 recon is 14–40% of the car's value, making replacement viable in most cases. A well‑maintained, lower‑mileage example justifies replacement; a tired, high‑mileage one may not.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes CLA35 AMG engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The CLA35 AMG engine code is M260 — a 2.0‑litre turbocharged inline‑4. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent the common failures on a CLA35 AMG?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — regular oil changes with Mercedes‑approved MB 229.5 5W‑30 oil every 10,000 km are the most effective prevention. On early cars, have the timing chain tensioner inspected at 40,000 miles. At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, misfire or rough idle, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes CLA35 AMG Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current M260 generation. A replacement M260 engine costs on average £4,500–£7,000 for a reconditioned unit, with new units from the main dealer significantly higher.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a CLA35 AMG engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

cta.label: → Compare CLA35 AMG engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start (often lasting a few seconds), check engine light with timing‑related fault codes, engine misfires, and in severe cases, loss of power if the chain jumps.
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£3,000 (chain, tensioner and guide rails replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The M260's timing chain tensioner is a known weak point. Rattling on cold start indicates that the tensioner is failing to maintain proper oil pressure, allowing the chain to slap against its guides. If left unaddressed, the chain can jump timing or snap, resulting in catastrophic engine damage. Regular oil changes with Mercedes‑approved synthetic oil (MB 229.5) every 10,000 km are essential. At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, have the timing chain system inspected immediately.

problems.title: Cylinder Head / Valve Guide Wear
problems.symptoms: Rough running, loss of compression, persistent misfires (even after replacing plugs and coils), high emissions, and in severe cases, coolant and oil mixing.
problems.repairCost: £2,500–£4,500 (cylinder head replacement with updated parts)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £4,500–£8,000 (full engine replacement if damage is extensive)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you experience misfires or rough running, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The M260 has a known defect in the valve heads — increased wear on exhaust valve seat rings and valve guides can cause insufficiently sealed combustion chambers. This leads to rough running, misfires and high emissions. Mercedes issued a warranty extension for this issue in some markets (ECR‑M0002‑22‑010). If you experience persistent misfires, have the cylinder head inspected immediately — catching it early can save you from a £4,500+ repair bill.

problems.title: Camtronic System Failure
problems.symptoms: Loss of low‑end power, rough idle, hesitation on acceleration, engine warning light, and in rare cases, complete failure of the variable valve lift system.
problems.repairCost: £1,000–£2,500 (Camtronic solenoid or actuator replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear

problems.recommendation: The CAMTRONIC system — which provides variable valve timing — is a rare but expensive failure point on the M260. If you notice a loss of low‑end power or rough running, have the CAMTRONIC system diagnosed. Replacing a failed solenoid or actuator is significantly cheaper than the performance loss and potential engine damage that can result from ignoring the issue.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: CLA35 AMG — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — before chain jumps
Replace with updated parts — permanent fix
Replace solenoid or actuator — cheaper than ignoring it

rows.problem: Severe engine damage (chain snap / valve failure)
rows.whenItMakesSense: Little left to salvage — replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This CLA35 AMG? Check These First
buyIf: * No cold‑start rattle — timing chain tensioner in good condition
* No misfire history — cylinder head in good condition
* Full Mercedes‑Benz service history with MB 229.5 oil spec
* 2021+ model year — later cars may have fewer early‑production issues
avoidIf: * Cold‑start rattle with no tensioner history — timing chain may be failing — walk away
* Misfire history with no resolution — cylinder head may be failing
* No service history — M260 engines are sensitive to neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The CLA35 AMG is the more accessible AMG — 306 hp, 4MATIC AWD and daily‑driver usability in a compact, stylish package. The M260 engine is technologically advanced but has known weaknesses: timing chain tensioner failure, cylinder head/valve guide wear, Camtronic failures and carbon buildup. Buy on full service history with evidence of regular oil changes (every 10,000 km), prioritise later‑model cars, and never ignore a cold‑start rattle or misfire.

cta.label: → Compare CLA35 AMG engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes CLA35 AMG (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling; timing chain tensioner indicator
* Misfire / Rough Running — Check for uneven running; cylinder head or Camtronic indicator
* Service History — including engine era (M260) and confirmed oil‑change intervals (10,000 km / MB 229.5 spec)
* Carbon Build‑up — Check for rough idle or hesitation; may need walnut blasting
* Oil Leaks — Check under the car and around the engine; valve cover and Camtronic seals are common leak points
* Turbo Health — Listen for whistling or whining; turbo actuator issues possible
* DCT7 Gearbox — Check for hesitation or harsh shifts; AMG Speedshift DCT requires regular service
* MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures
